title: Slatewave CLI manifest
description: Per-theme manifest consumed by the slatewave CLI. Each shipping Slatewave theme has one of these in slatewave-cli/internal/manifest/embedded/<slug>.toml. Mirrors the Theme struct in slatewave-cli/internal/manifest/manifest.go.

slug.description: Canonical CLI identifier (e.g. "bat", "btop"). Must match the manifest's filename (<slug>.toml) and the slug on getslatewave.com.

category.description: Category, mirroring the website's category enum.

detect_command.description: Shell command run to verify the underlying tool is installed before any install action. Non-zero exit → CLI errors with "<tool> not detected" without modifying any files.

detect_command_windows.description: Windows-specific override of detect_command. Used when the unix detect uses POSIX-only forms like `command -v <tool>` and a cmd.exe-parseable equivalent is needed (`where <tool>`, `<tool> --version`, etc.). Empty → fall back to detect_command.

supported_os.description: OS values from runtime.GOOS this manifest claims to work on. Empty/unset defaults to ["darwin", "linux"] — pre-Windows behavior. Cross-platform manifests must list "windows" explicitly so that an unaudited manifest can't accidentally be offered to (and fail mid-install on) Windows users.

version_regex.description: Regex applied to detect_command stdout to extract the installed tool's version. Capture group 1 must hold an X.Y.Z version. Required when install.variants is non-empty; otherwise unused. Lets one manifest dispatch its install URL based on the installed tool's version (e.g. lsd 1.0.x silently ignores hex colors and needs an ANSI-256 variant).

install.description: How to put the theme files in place.

type.description: Install dispatch tag. Each value maps to a function in internal/installer.

dest.description: Destination path on disk (curl, gui-import). Supports ~ and $ENV expansion.

files.description: Multi-file curl install (curl). When set, url+dest must be empty. Each entry is fetched in order; each dest is recorded as a CreatedPath so uninstall reverses cleanly. Used when one theme ships multiple assets that must land alongside the user's config (e.g. wezterm's slatewave-full.lua + slatewave.lua dependency).

clone_dest.description: Where to clone the repo (clone). Supports ~ and $ENV expansion. Used as fallback when no per-OS override matches.

clone_dest_darwin.description: macOS-specific clone destination. Takes precedence over clone_dest when running on darwin.

clone_dest_linux.description: Linux-specific clone destination. Takes precedence over clone_dest when running on linux.

clone_dest_windows.description: Windows-specific clone destination. Takes precedence over clone_dest when running on windows.

cli.description: Override the binary the vscode-ext handler shells out to. Defaults to "code". Set to "cursor" for Cursor, "codium" for VSCodium, etc. Every editor in the family accepts the same `--install-extension` / `--list-extensions` / `--uninstall-extension` flags, so only the binary name varies.

instructions.description: Lines printed verbatim after the install runs. Required when install.type = "manual".

description.description: Step label shown to the user ("Rebuilding bat cache").

command.description: Shell command to run after install completes.

done_message.description: Success line printed after a non-bulk install completes ("bat picks up the new theme on its next invocation.", "Restart your shell or `source` your rc file."). Omit to use the generic "Slatewave is installed." default.

done_message_windows.description: Windows-specific override of done_message. Most commonly used for shell-rc activates targeting a PowerShell profile, where users hit Set-ExecutionPolicy before the appended line runs. Empty / unset → fall back to done_message.

variants.description: Version-conditional install overrides. When a variant's when_version matches the version captured via theme.version_regex, its url/dest/files override the parent fields (first-match-wins). Lets one manifest target multiple incompatible tool versions — e.g. lsd 1.0.x needs an ANSI-256 colors file because it silently ignores hex; 1.1+ wants the hex file. Requires theme.version_regex to be set.

when_version.description: Constraint expression: "<X.Y.Z", "<=X.Y.Z", ">X.Y.Z", ">=X.Y.Z", "=X.Y.Z", or bare "X.Y.Z" (treated as exact match). Anything else is rejected at install time.

url.description: Override for install.url when this variant matches. Omit to inherit from the parent.

dest.description: Override for install.dest when this variant matches. Omit to inherit.

files.description: Override for install.files when this variant matches. When set, the parent's url/dest are cleared so dispatch sees a clean multi-file shape.

activate.description: How to flip the user's config to point at the installed theme. Empty/none means the install step also activates.

value.description: Value to set the key to (ini-key).

files.description: Candidate rc / config files in priority order (shell-rc). The activator picks the first existing one, falling back to $SHELL match, then the first candidate.

files_windows.description: PowerShell-profile equivalents of `files`, used when runtime.GOOS == "windows" (shell-rc). A manifest declaring supported_os = [..., "windows"] must set both files_windows and line_windows together — without them the activator errors cleanly rather than appending bash syntax to a .ps1 file.

scaffold.description: Full file contents written when the chosen target is missing or whitespace-only (shell-rc). For configs where appending one line to an empty file produces invalid syntax (e.g. wezterm.lua: `config.color_scheme = ...` requires `local config = wezterm.config_builder()` first). Should already include the activation line — `line` is only re-appended when the file already had real content.

insert_before.description: Substring matched against trimmed line content (shell-rc). When set on a non-empty file, `line` is spliced just above the first matching line instead of being appended at EOF. Use for configs where the appended line would be unreachable — e.g. wezterm.lua's `return config` halts execution. Falls back to append when the anchor isn't found.

comment_prefix.description: Comment-syntax prefix for the marker comment we write above the activation line (shell-rc). Defaults to `#` — correct for shell rcs, gitconfig, ssh config. Lua targets must set this to `--` so the marker is a valid comment in the host file.

toml_path.description: TOML file to add an import entry to (toml-import). Supports ~ and $ENV expansion.

import.description: Path to add to the import = [...] array (toml-import).

yaml_path.description: YAML config file to edit (yaml-set). Supports ~ and $ENV expansion.

yaml_set.description: Nested-key sets to apply to the YAML file (yaml-set). Each entry is a depth-2 dotted path "parent.child" plus the scalar value to write under it.

value.description: Scalar value to write under the path.

verify.description: Optional smoke-test that runs after install (and is also re-run by `slatewave list` to detect drift).

command.description: Shell command. Non-zero exit means the install no longer detectable.

command_windows.description: Windows-specific override of command. Empty → fall back to command. Use when the unix verify shells out to `test -f` / `grep` / etc. and a cmd.exe-parseable equivalent is needed.

expect.description: Optional substring expected in the command's stdout.

trust_state.description: When true, doctor + list reconcile skip verify.command and trust the state record. Use only when post-install storage is opaque to us (Alfred imports a .alfredappearance into its own UUID-named subdir; the transient ~/Downloads file isn't a reliable check).

done_message.description: Success line printed after `slatewave uninstall <slug>` completes ("Reverted. Quit and relaunch Ghostty to see your original colors..."). Omit to use the generic "Reverted." default. Set this for tools that load config once at launch (terminals, editor processes) so the user knows to relaunch.

done_message_windows.description: Windows-specific override of uninstall.done_message. Empty / unset → fall back to done_message. Same rationale as install.done_message_windows.
